obligation_extraction.obligation: A Swiss QR-bill must include both a receipt and a payment section.
The Swiss QR Code must contain payment information needed for invoicing and payment.
Visible payment information must be available in plain text so the recipient can check it before approval.
For payments with a structured QR reference, the credited account must be a QR-IBAN.
A QR-IBAN can only be used for invoicing and payments in CHF in this QR-bill context.
The QR reference must have 26 numerical characters followed by a modulo 10 recursive check digit.
The QR reference must not consist exclusively of zeros.
A QR reference can only be used in combination with a QR-IBAN.
A Creditor Reference can only be used with an IBAN for invoicing in CHF or EUR.
QR-bills must use structured address elements in the Swiss QR Code under the November 2025 requirements.
Faulty Swiss QR Code printing can disrupt processing and payment parts can be rejected.
The product guarantees every QR-bill will be accepted by all banks automatically.

traceability_matrix.obligation: For payments with a structured QR reference, the credited account must be a QR-IBAN.
traceability_matrix.validation_rule: Block QR reference with standard IBAN; require QR-IBAN when structured QR reference is used.

traceability_matrix.obligation: The QR reference must have 26 numerical characters followed by a modulo 10 recursive check digit.
traceability_matrix.validation_rule: Validate 26 numeric characters plus checksum placeholder before export.

traceability_matrix.obligation: A Creditor Reference can only be used with an IBAN for invoicing in CHF or EUR.
traceability_matrix.validation_rule: Block Creditor Reference when no standard IBAN is provided; permit CHF/EUR only.

qa_test_cases.scenario: Negative: reject QR reference used with a standard IBAN instead of QR-IBAN.
Negative: reject QR reference with all zeros or wrong length/check digit.
Negative: reject Creditor Reference not starting with RF or used without IBAN.
Negative: warn when PDF-to-paper processing requirements are not documented.
